_provenance.stage: Stage 1 — derived from live cards for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Vernacular Schools Serve 1.3 Million Students Amid Funding Disparity
context: Malaysia's 1,302 Chinese and 529 Tamil vernacular schools serve 1.3 million students but receive 38% less per-student federal funding than national schools.

cards.text: 1.3 million Malaysian children attend vernacular schools.
cards.sub: Federal per-student allocation is RM4,200 for national schools and RM2,600 for vernacular schools — a 38% gap.

cards.h: Article 152(1)(a) guarantees the right to use and teach other languages.
cards.s: Vernacular education is constitutionally protected, not a concession.
cards.d: Article 152(1)(a) guarantees the right to use and teach other languages. Vernacular education is constitutionally protected, not a concession.

cards.h: 96% of SJKC funding gaps are filled by community donations.
cards.s: Chinese school boards raised RM1.8B in private funds between 2020 and 2024.
cards.d: 96% of SJKC funding gaps are filled by community donations. Chinese school boards raised RM1.8B in private funds between 2020 and 2024.

cards.h: 28% of SJKC students are non-Chinese, including 12% Malay.
cards.s: Vernacular schools increasingly serve multiethnic populations — undermining the 'divisive' narrative.
cards.d: 28% of SJKC students are non-Chinese, including 12% Malay. Vernacular schools increasingly serve multiethnic populations — undermining the 'divisive' narrative.

cards.text: If vernacular schools are divisive, why are 28% of students from other communities choosing them?
Equal funding for constitutionally guaranteed education is not a favour — it is an obligation. The funding gap should be closed regardless of one's position on school structure.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
